## Semantic-release intent

Choose the commit/release type this PR should produce.

- [ ] `fix`: patch release
- [ ] `feat`: minor release
- [ ] `docs`: documentation-only
- [ ] `refactor`: internal code restructuring
- [ ] `perf`: performance improvement
- [ ] `test`: tests only
- [ ] `build`: build or dependency tooling change
- [ ] `ci`: CI or workflow change
- [ ] `chore`: maintenance with no user-facing behavior change
- [ ] Breaking change (`!` or `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer)

Suggested PR title prefix: `fix:`, `feat:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `perf:`, `test:`, `build:`, `ci:`, or `chore:`

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] CI/CD or workflow update
- [ ] Breaking change

## Related issue

Closes #

## Implementation notes

Call out important design choices, tradeoffs, or migration details.

## Validation

List the commands you ran and their results.

```bash
npm run -s lint
npm run -s typecheck
npm run -s test:unit
```

## GitHub ruleset compliance

- [ ] Required status checks pass.
- [ ] Required reviews are complete.
- [ ] Branch is up to date with base branch requirements.
- [ ] Workflow/action updates keep pinned commit SHAs.

## Release impact

- [ ] No user-facing changes
- [ ] Patch release
- [ ] Minor release
- [ ] Major release

## API and compatibility

- [ ] Public API unchanged
- [ ] Public API changed and documented
- [ ] Backward compatibility impact reviewed

## Checklist

- [ ] I followed the [repository contribution guidelines](https://github.com/awalsh128/ts-apt/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [ ] I added or updated tests where needed.
- [ ] I updated docs or comments where needed.
- [ ] I verified CI-related changes use pinned action SHAs.
- [ ] I verified no secrets were introduced.
